ncs_standard_definition.description_short: A Sericulturist is responsible for the cultivation of silkworms and the production of silk. They oversee the entire sericulture process, from mulberry cultivation to cocoon harvesting and silk reeling.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: Sericulturists work in sericulture farms, research institutions, or government organizations. Their primary responsibilities include managing mulberry plantations, breeding silkworms, monitoring cocoon quality, and ensuring efficient silk production. They must also stay updated on the latest sericulture techniques, technologies, and market trends.
ncs_standard_definition.official_min_education: B.Sc in Sericulture or related field

first_stepper.description: High school students interested in agriculture and sericulture can pursue a degree in Sericulture or a related field.

choice_maker.description: Individuals with a generic degree in agriculture or a related field can pursue a certification or a bridge course in sericulture to transition into this role.

future_builder.description: Experienced Sericulturists can advance to senior roles by acquiring advanced skills in sericulture technology, business management, or research and development.
future_builder.prerequisite_experience: 2-3 years of experience as a Sericulturist

problem_solver_uturn.description: Individuals from unrelated fields can transition into sericulture by acquiring relevant skills and experience. No degree is required, but a strong portfolio and survival skills are essential.
